question.title: How to compare the URL with pathVariable in Java
question.body:
I am keeping /{id}/request in the property file, and I will get /3210/request as a string value from the request. Could you please help to achieve this?

I have tried the below pattern match, and it's working fine, but I want to know if there is any other better way to achieve this.

boolean regex="/1234/request".matches("/\\d{4,5}/request");

answers.body:
How to compare the URL with pathVariable in Java (with Regex) ?

One practice is to use combination of Matcher.find() and Matcher.group(), when you perform a match on a string using a regular expression pattern and a Matcher object, the group() method returns the substring that was matched by the entire pattern, as an instance i'm interested in request (note that not /request)

String url = "https://ireallydontcare.com/request";
String pathVariable = "request";
String regex = "^https:\\/\\/ireallydontcare\\.com\\/(.*)$";
Pattern pattern = Pattern.compile(regex);
Matcher matcher = pattern.matcher(url);
return matcher.find() && matcher.group(1).equals(pathVariable)

Also the @RequestMapping can include regular expressions to match specific URL patterns.

@GetMapping("/{key:\\d(4,5)}/request")
public String getUserById(@PathVariable("key") String key) {
    ...
}
